business_meaning: Net Income — bottom-line profit (losses signal distress)
Retained Earnings — accumulated profits (negative = history of losses)
Receivables — amounts owed to the firm
Return on Assets — profitability relative to asset base
Return on Equity — profitability for shareholders
Cash Ratio — most conservative liquidity measure
Interest Coverage — ability to service debt (lower = riskier)
Fed Funds Rate (lagged 1yr) — monetary policy tightness
Baa Corporate Yield (lagged) — credit market risk appetite
Aaa Corporate Yield (lagged) — high-grade borrowing cost
S&P/CapIQ Credit Rating (numeric scale — higher = worse)
Years Since Last Rating — stale ratings may miss deterioration
Log(Total Assets) — firm size (larger firms default less)
Firm Age in Years — younger firms are riskier
Total Debt / Total Assets — core leverage ratio
(Debt - Cash) / Assets — leverage net of cash buffer
Debt / EBITDA — years to repay debt from earnings
Working Capital / Assets — short-term liquidity buffer (Altman Z1)
Retained Earnings / Assets — cumulative profitability (Altman Z2)
Book Equity / Liabilities — solvency ratio (Altman Z4 book version)
EBITDA / Interest Expense — debt servicing capacity
Free Cash Flow (Operating CF - CapEx) — cash generation
Free Cash Flow / Total Debt — ability to repay from cash flow
Operating Cash Flow / Assets — cash-based profitability
Year-over-Year Revenue Growth — declining revenue signals distress
Baa - Aaa Yield Spread (lagged) — credit cycle indicator
Merton Distance-to-Default — structural credit metric (higher = safer)
Prediction Horizon in Months — time window for default probability
